column2: Description (This field is optional, you can leave it blank if you want to.)
column3: Document Content (Mandatory, set one of the following values: 0 for Use Content, 1 for Use Attachments, 2 for Use URL)
column4: Content Editor Text (This field is mandatory only if Document Content is set to "Use Content".)
column5: URL (This field is mandatory only if Document Content is set to "URL".)
column6: GRC Contact (Mandatory. Accepts multiple user logins or group names separated by "|". For User login use prefix "User-" and for Group name use "Group-". For example "User-admin|Group-Third Party Feedback|Group-Admin". You can get the login of an user account from System / Settings / User Management or name of a group from System / Settings / Groups.)
column7: Policy Reviewer Contact (Mandatory. Accepts multiple user logins or group names separated by "|". For User login use prefix "User-" and for Group name use "Group-". For example "User-admin|Group-Third Party Feedback|Group-Admin". You can get the login of an user account from System / Settings / User Management or name of a group from System / Settings / Groups.)
column8: Tags (Optional and accepts tags separated by "|". For example "Critical|SOX|PCI".)
column9: Published Date (Mandatory, it must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD, not the "-" character is used as delimiter.)
column10: Next Review Date (Mandatory, it must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD, not the "-" character is used as delimiter.)
column11: Policy Labels (Optional, set the value of the name of the label you want to use. Label names can be obtained from Asset Management / Asset Identification / Settings / Labels.)
column12: Related Projects (Optional and accepts multiple names separated by "|". You need to enter the name of a project, you can find them at Security Operations / Project Management.)
column13: Status (Mandatory, set value: 0 for Draft, 1 for Published)
column14: Document Type (You need to provide the name of the policy type, you can obtain the name of type from Control Catalogue / Policies / Settings / Document Type.)
column16: Policy Portal Permissions for this Document (Mandatory, set one of the following values: public for Public (Everyone can see the document), private for Private (Document is not shown on the portal), custom-roles for Only specific groups)
column17: Custom Roles (Mandatory in case you set permission to custom-roles, you can set more of following values: Owners for GRC Contact, Collaborators for Policy Reviewer Contact)
column18: Related Documents (Optional and accepts multiple names separated by "|". You need to enter the name of a policy, you can find them at Control Catalogue / Policies.)

column4:
Introduction


This Asset Management Policy provides a framework for the management and protection of organizational assets, ensuring efficiency, security, and compliance with relevant regulations and standards.

Scope


• This policy applies to all organizational assets, whether physical or digital.
• All staff members, contractors, and third parties engaging with the organization are obligated to adhere to this policy.

Assets & Standards


Assets under this policy are classified into Physical Assets, Data Assets, and Software Licenses. The standards set forth the responsibilities and guidelines for the acquisition, maintenance, protection, and disposal of these assets.

Asset Types


Physical Assets


• All physical assets must be recorded in the Inventory of Assets with a unique identifier.
• Assets should be securely stored and maintained. Damage or loss should be reported immediately to the IT department.
• Use of organization-owned assets for non-business purposes should be minimal and compliant with the IT Security Policy.
• Disposal or re-allocation of assets should be coordinated through the IT department, ensuring data security and proper documentation.

Data Assets


• Access control measures must be in place, ensuring only authorized individuals have access to specific data assets.
• Regular backup and recovery procedures should be implemented for critical data.
• Data retention and destruction must align with the organization's established policies and regulatory requirements.

Software Licenses


• The organization will maintain a Software License Registry to track, validate, and renew licenses.
• Staff are required to follow guidelines when installing software, ensuring compliance and avoiding potential security threats.

Acceptable Use of Information and Associated Assets


Expected Behaviors


All individuals accessing or using the organization's information and associated assets must adhere to the highest standards of integrity, confidentiality, and responsibility. Any behavior compromising the security, integrity, or confidentiality of these assets is strictly prohibited.

Permitted and Prohibited Uses


• Permitted Uses: Accessing and using assets strictly for business purposes, following established security protocols and operational guidelines.
• Prohibited Uses: Utilizing assets for unauthorized purposes, including but not limited to personal benefit, illegal activities, or any actions that could harm the organization or its reputation.

Monitoring and Accountability


• The organization reserves the right to monitor the use of its information and associated assets to ensure compliance with this policy.
• Individuals found in violation of these guidelines may face disciplinary action up to and including termination of employment or legal action.

Information Lifecycle Management


• Access to information and assets will be governed by their classification level and the associated risks.
• Proper records of authorized users, asset allocation, and disposal methodologies will be maintained.

Storage and Marking


• All assets must be stored and maintained as per manufacturer specifications and organizational standards.
• Storage media containing sensitive information must be clearly marked and handled only by authorized personnel.

Disposal and Deletion


The disposal of information and associated assets must be authorized and conducted using methods that ensure the complete and secure deletion of sensitive information.

Ownership of Assets


Assets provided by the organization are its exclusive property. Unauthorized sale, transfer, or loan of these assets is strictly prohibited.

Provisioning and Return of Assets


• Assets should be used responsibly, following IT Security and organizational policies.
• The IT department will oversee asset allocation, ensuring alignment with business needs and security requirements.
• Employees must return all organization-owned assets upon termination of employment or when no longer needed.

Changes to the Inventory of Assets


• The IT department holds the authority to propose and implement changes to the asset inventory.
• Major changes require thorough documentation, capturing the reasons, discussions, and decisions made during the change process. Minor updates might be tracked through document revision history.

Monitoring and Review


• All stakeholders should actively ensure compliance with this policy.
• Periodic audits, spearheaded by the IT department, will ensure asset integrity, accuracy of the inventory, and adherence to the policy.
